Mechanism: corpus tiers with annealing and transfer probes

Three tiers: T1, a fresh unconstrained conversation-heavy language substrate that never falls below a 10-15 percent floor; T2, variable-length simulation traffic freshly sampled per epoch; T3, real operational data held out as never-trained transfer probes.

T1 is generated with the LLM deliberately unconstrained (constraint is what collapsed diversity before) and replaces all prior corpora, which are retired to baselines. T2 anneals until the operational register dominates, which sets the default register, and stops on metric criteria rather than a token budget. T3 (public Apollo loop transcripts, ATC phraseology, real arithmetic word problems) is measurement first: held out to answer whether simulation-taught logic transferred. Register discipline is scored asymmetrically: operational outputs must pass field-completeness and brevity budgets, chat outputs need only coherence.

The tier ordering encodes the training sequence: language comprehension first, then the simulation, then real data as the test. The T1 floor is what makes degradation graceful, keeping plain conversation in-distribution as a capability while operations stays the default; the general-language fragility gauge (bare-greeting inputs) is a first-class evaluation axis so the schema-collapse failure cannot hide again. T2 stopping criteria at the iteration rung: decision exact-match at least 0.8 with counterfactual consistency at least 0.7 sustained across two fresh corpora, or a plateau over three consecutive fresh epochs; transfer probes are measured at every checkpoint but gate the approach, not the stage. Kind-differentiated SFT (node 52) survives as the mechanism for reserved evaluation kinds, and the worldgen puzzle generators survive as the arithmetic slice inside T2, the trained counterpart of the T3 arithmetic transfer probe. A small T3 finetune is a later, separate decision.
